pattern: Because only the remainder modulo k matters, the repunit's remainder evolves as r becomes (r*10 + 1) mod k each step, so you walk that chain until it hits zero or a cycle repeats.
hints: You never need the huge repunit itself — track only its remainder modulo k, which is all that decides divisibility.
Appending a one to the repunit transforms the remainder r into (r * 10 + 1) mod k; iterate this and count steps.
If k is even or ends in five it can never divide a number ending in one, so return minus one; otherwise the remainder must reach zero within k steps, and the step count is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: k is one, single digit works
even k can never divide a repunit
a coprime value needing many digits
near the upper bound, coprime to ten

stress.description: random k across the range, including impossible and long-chain cases
